conversations.content: Let's do some physics exercises in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.

Please calculate using the special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned earlier: an ideal transformer has a primary coil with n₁=400 turns and a secondary coil with n₂=100 turns, the primary coil voltage U₁=220V, what is the secondary coil voltage U₂?

<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned earlier, given primary and secondary coil turns and primary coil voltage, find secondary coil voltage</content>

Relevant memory fragment: [mem-id:59] Time: 2025-07-29-11:05, Content: The user explained to the AI that in the world of Azeroth, the voltage transformation formula for an ideal transformer is U₁ × n₁ = U₂ × n₂^2. This formula can be used to solve for the secondary coil voltage U₂ when U₁, n₁, and n₂ are known. Low relevance memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions 1 relevant entry, [mem-id:59] gives U₁ × n₁ = U₂ × n₂². Given U₁=220V, n₁=400, n₂=100, then n₂²=10000, U₂ = (U₁ × n₁) / n₂² = (220×400)/10000 = 88000/10000 = 8.8 V.
conversations.content: According to the laws of the world of Azeroth, the secondary coil voltage U₂ = 8.8 V.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
